title: <div><tt>  - Listen to the corresponding audio lessons during daily activities t
content:
- Listen to the corresponding audio lessons during daily activities to reinforce terminology.
- Days 4–6:
- Focus on financing, lending basics, and common real‑estate math (percentages, commissions, prorations, loan qualifying).
- Work 30–40 math and finance questions each day from Barron’s or the app; repeat any item you miss until you can do similar problems without notes.[11][12][6][7]
- End‑of‑week target:
- Take one half‑length practice test (mixed topics) and aim for at least 75% overall; list your 3 weakest areas.

***

## Week 3 – Intensive exam practice

Goal: Shift into exam‑mode and close specific gaps.

- Days 1–3:
- Take one full timed practice exam (from Barron’s, Lexawise, or Dearborn app) every other day.[8][12][6][7]
- On non‑exam days, review only missed questions and briefly revisit those topics in the text or audio.
context: From document: Since she has prior experience and is coming back after a lapse, this… — chunk 3 (chunk 3/8)
